name: Dan Barrett
abstract: Dan Barrett (born December 14, 1955, in Pasadena, California) is an American arranger, cornetist, and trombonist. The earliest mention of Dan Barrett was in the Melody Maker, 10 February 1973, which reported that he played "Ory's Creole Trombone" at the end of Kid Ory's Funeral on 28 January that year.

interviews.title: Dan Barrett interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: For the Love of Louis
Carnegie Hall Notables: The Ninth Annual Notable Occasion: Rossano Sportiello and His American Jazz Stars

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: Dan Barrett is a colleague and a good friend and a marvelous player. I love playing with Dan because we work well off one another.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: I co-led a band with a trombonist Dan Barrett off and on for a number of years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Mat Domber
about_quotes.evidence: So I said yeah, let’s do the record with Dan Barrett. So we did and through Dan, Dan became our music director for a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Mat Domber interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Evan Christopher
about_quotes.evidence: I knew who Dan was because he was from southern California, Dan Barrett. And I think Dan and I had met on the bandstand a couple of times in California... friends of ours: Dan Barrett
about_quotes.doc_title: Evan Christopher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 1, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: they happen to be the ones that I’ve been working with lately. But there are young guys that are marvelous players... Dan Barrett, Jon-Erik Kellso, Randy Sandke, Harry Allen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: Well I’ve got so much respect for Dan Barrett that I would tend to listen to you, Dan. [INTERVIEWER] Thank you. You know the feeling is mutual, and it has been a long time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
